description_long: This is a tall, tapered stone tower with a wide, textured base that narrows as it rises. The base appears to be constructed from rough, brownish stone blocks, giving it a sturdy and ancient look. At the top, the structure transitions into a lighter, smoother section with vertical slits or windows, capped by a domed roof. The dome is a reddish-brown color and is supported by evenly spaced columns or bars, creating an open, lantern-like effect. The tower has a single, small entrance at the bottom center of the base, suggesting limited access. The overall design is reminiscent of historical or commemorative towers found in urban parks or plazas.
description: A tall, stone-based tower with a domed, columned top and a single entrance at the base, featuring rough stonework and a lantern-like upper section.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance at the base and the full height of the tower, with the domed top and vertical slits clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the entrance less visible, highlighting the tapering stone base and the cylindrical upper section with the dome.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but without a visible entrance, showing the symmetry of the stonework and the domed top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again with no visible entrance, emphasizing the tower's taper and the open, columned dome structure.
